category_title: Students with untenable mainstream histories exhibiting workplace-related non-compliance like lateness
minimal_context: The student is arriving at the TAFE classroom at the start of the day and is expected to use the sign-in sheet.
dialogue:
TUTOR: It is the start of the day. Please use the sign-in sheet.

STUDENT: (Walks in late, speaking at a high decibel) Yeah, yeah, I made it.

TUTOR: You still need to sign in on the sheet.

STUDENT: I don't even want to be here. This place is just a shelter from unemployment.

TUTOR: Please follow the classroom expectations and sign in.

STUDENT: I forgot to sign in. I'm tired from moving house.

options.A: Implement classroom routines, such as signing in and out each day, that mimic workplace practices.
options.B: Ensure a reinforcer is only offered after the desired sign-in behavior has occurred, and not at other times.
options.C: Use a simple checklist to document the student's pragmatic language during different classroom situations throughout the day.
options.D: Review a behavior tracking sheet, have the student count the positive and negative marks, and discuss their overall day.
